response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with ~15 years of experience and genuine strengths in high-throughput distributed systems (real-time ad-tech services handling millions of requests per second) and developer mentoring, but he clearly misses the core minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ — his C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, with his systems work done primarily in DLang and TypeScript/Node.js. He also lacks demonstrated production cloud-platform architecture ownership and formal technical-leadership experience managing cross-team priorities, making him a weak fit for this specific C++-centric compute-platform lead role despite strong overall engineering craftsmanship.
strengths: Hands-on experience with low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Long, varied engineering career spanning web, embedded, and infrastructure with strong testing/TDD discipline
Proven mentor and knowledge-sharer (workshops, courses, conference talks, open-source maintainership)
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience
No evidence of owning production cloud compute platform architecture at scale
No formal technical-leadership role leading strategy, priorities, and cross-team alignment
